name: Fried Daing na Bangus
description: a popular Filipino dish featuring crispy, golden-brown fish, often prepared by marinating boneless milkfish in vinegar, garlic, salt, and peppercorns.

name: Fried Pompano
description: a dish featuring the pompano fish, a marine fish known for its firm, white, and mildly sweet meat, which is deep-fried or pan-fried until the skin is crispy and the inside is soft and juicy.

name: Fried Tilapya
description: a popular dish featuring tilapia fish seasoned and pan-fried or deep-fried until golden, crispy on the outside, and tender on the inside.

name: Grilled Bangus
description: a popular Filipino dish consisting of milkfish—the national fish of the Philippines—that is butterflied, stuffed with tomatoes, onions, and ginger, then grilled over charcoal until smoky and charred.

name: Fried Galunggong (5pcs)
description: a popular dish featuring galunggong fish seasoned and pan-fried or deep-fried until golden, crispy on the outside, and tender on the inside.

name: Adobong Pusit
description: a classic Filipino dish consisting of small squid stewed in soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, onions, and its own black ink.

name: Seafood Boil
description: a social dining experience and cooking method where shellfish (shrimp, mussles, crab, ), corn and potatoes are boiled together in a large pot with spices.
